rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALBERT WITT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. However, prior to final distribution, the executor must address: (1) under what authority she paid her former attorney $25,000 in fees [CRC 7.700, 7.704, Probate Code Sect. 10814], and the order dated 8/24/21, which specifically ordered that "No attorney fees can be paid without a prior court order."]; (2) fully disclose all claims filed, including the one filed on 8/3/18, and what actions she has taken on such claim(s); and (3) correct the date of her appointment (12/7/16, not 12/8/16) and the date Letters Testamentary were issued (5/26/17, not 12/8/16). Paragraphs 14 and 31 of this petition have contradictory allegations. The Final I&A filed on 2/1/19 was already for values as of the date of death; it is unclear why the properties need to be reappraised as of Decedent's death, as alleged in paragraph 9 of this report. Paragraph 10 of this report was also incomplete regarding the date on which a Notice of Proposed Action was reportedly filed. The executor being the sole beneficiary is the salient point; her being the sole heir has no bearing, as the estate will be distributed pursuant to the will. Status of closing the estate will be set for 3/8/23. A petition for final distribution or a fourth report of status must be filed by 2/22/23. Appearance will be required on 3/8/23 if no petition is filed by 2/22/23.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE BENJAMIN M. PICETTI 2007 REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Petition For Order To Charge Partners Interest
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/21/23 per judicial officer. The court does not understand this petition to be properly filed in the probate division. Petitioner is instructed to file a memorandum of points and authorities explaining why the petition can/should be heard in the probate division, rather than the civil division. In addition, if the petition is properly before the probate division, it must be noticed as a trust petition and proof of proper notice and service must be filed. All required documents to be filed and provided by 2/6/23.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HENRY Z. HELMER SR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/8/2023. (1) Need clarification if the Disney Retirement Benefit is a saving plan that should have been appraised by the Probate Referee or is in cash. I&A filed on 8/19/22 merely set forth as Disney Retirement Plan without specific form of the asset. If it should have been appraised by the probate referee, the I&A must be resubmitted. (2) Petition and proposed order to describe the proposed distribution in compliance with CRC 7.651. Also, the distribution needs to state the dollar amount (if it is in cash) the beneficiary will ultimately receive after the fees and the costs are fully paid.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: HIROYUKI KAJIMA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Order To Confirm Personal Property To Petitioner, Wife
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/28/23 by the judicial officer. The petitioner was directed to seek relief in an estate proceeding after appointment of a personal representative, among other things. Petitioner has not made an affirmative showing of Decedent's intent. The personal representative cannot be the petitioner, who seeks relief from the estate. If a personal representative cannot be appointed in time for an amended petition to be filed and served on the personal representative, Counsel should contact the examiner to request additional time.

For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE C.I. SETTLEMENT PROTECTION TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Establish Minor'S Settlement Trust; To Set Bond; Allow For Interim Fees; And To Fix And Allow Attorneys' Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/21/23 by the judicial officer, who specifically directed that the trust is to remain under Court supervision until the beneficiary turns 18. The Court is not inclined to appoint an out-of-state trustee or to approve a 1.5% fee, especially without any indication as to how assets will be invested, and the trust itself, not just the court's order, must clearly provide that the beneficiary will have 30 days to revoke the trust after she receives a copy of the trust and written notice of her right to revoke the trust after she turns 18. The address of the trust protector must be set forth in another supplement unless the petitioner provides a reason why it does not.

Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BEATRIZ QUITOLES
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/22/23. (No earlier availability.) No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] No courtesy copies. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. A proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and all supporting papers must be received by the assigned examiner by 2/10/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM COLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/14/23 at Counsel's request to publish notice. (No earlier date available.) Petition has not yet been fully reviewed. No proof of service has been filed either, as of 1/3/23. Item 5a(7) or (8) of the petition must be answered. The Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities is incomplete (no telephone number). No proposed order has been submitted or received by the assigned examiner as of 1/3/23. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] Courtesy copies of all papers must be received by the assigned examiner by 2/3/23.
